title: Separate authentication from authorization
description: Proving who the principal is (authn) is kept distinct from what they may do (authz); a valid login is not access.
body: Use when a bug or feature blurs login and permission, e.g. 'logged-in users can reach admin'. Do not treat a valid session as sufficient for a privileged action. Actions: confirm the authz check runs after authn on every protected path; ensure elevation requires an explicit permission, not just a session. Validation signals include a test where an authenticated low-privilege user is denied a privileged action. Risk boundary: conflating authn and authz on a sensitive path is a blocking finding.
